title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for status lines, informational responses, content rules, and response parsing and requires the smallest strict response control-data and content decision parser with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for response control-data and content decision parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Parse HEAD response metadata as if GET were selected while never consuming response content bytes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Retry Ambiguity
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz protocol team handles case 6531 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Retry Ambiguity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify incomplete, malformed, unsolicited, and connection-lost responses without blindly replaying methods whose effects may have occurred. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Response Retry Ambiguity, keep Response Retry Ambiguity connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using classify and incomplete plus specific case evidence.
